title: Coprosma triflora
intro: Coprosma triflora, also known as New Zealand teatree, is a small evergreen shrub native to New Zealand, characterized by its bright green, leathery leaves and vibrant red berries. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Coprosma triflora include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Coprosma triflora include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory infections and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Coprosma triflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest families of flowering plants, with over 13,000 known species. The plants within this family are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world, although some species can be found in temperate zones. Coprosma triflora, like other members of the Rubiaceae family, is a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two seed leaves.
